nimbledeps/pkgs2/unittest2-0.2.5-02bb3751ba9ddc3c17bfd89f2e41cb6bfb8fc0c9/unittest2.nim
Line Branch Exec Source
1 # unittest2
2 #
3 # (c) Copyright 2015 Nim Contributors
4 # (c) Copyright 2019-2021 Ștefan Talpalaru
5 # (c) Copyright 2021-Onwards Status Research and Development
6 #
7
8 {.push raises: [].}
9
10 ## :Authors: Zahary Karadjov, Ștefan Talpalaru, Status Research and Development
11 ##
12 ## This module makes unit testing easy.
13 ##
14 ## .. code::
15 ## nim c -r testfile.nim
16 ##
17 ## exits with 0 or 1.
18 ##
19 ## Running individual tests
20 ## ========================
21 ##
22 ## Specify the test names as command line arguments.
23 ##
24 ## .. code::
25 ##
26 ## nim c -r test "my test name" "another test"
27 ##
28 ## Multiple arguments can be used.
29 ##
30 ## Running a single test suite
31 ## ===========================
32 ##
33 ## Specify the suite name delimited by ``"::"``.
34 ##
35 ## .. code::
36 ##
37 ## nim c -r test "my suite name::"
38 ##
39 ## Selecting tests by pattern
40 ## ==========================
41 ##
42 ## A single ``"*"`` can be used for globbing.
43 ##
44 ## Delimit the end of a suite name with ``"::"``.
45 ##
46 ## Tests matching **any** of the arguments are executed.
47 ##
48 ## .. code::
49 ##
50 ## nim c -r test fast_suite::mytest1 fast_suite::mytest2
51 ## nim c -r test "fast_suite::mytest*"
52 ## nim c -r test "auth*::" "crypto::hashing*"
53 ## # Run suites starting with 'bug #' and standalone tests starting with '#'
54 ## nim c -r test 'bug #*::' '::#*'
55 ##
56 ## Command line arguments
57 ## ======================
58 ##
59 ## The unit test runner recognises serveral parameters that can be specified
60 ## either via environment or command line, the latter taking precedence.
61 ##
62 ## Several options also have defaults that can be controlled at compile-time.
63 ##
64 ## --help Print short help and quit
65 ## --xml:file Write JUnit-compatible XML report to `file`
66 ## --console Write report to the console (default, when no other output
67 ## is selected)
68 ## --output-lvl:level Verbosity of output [COMPACT, VERBOSE, FAILURES, NONE] (env: UNITTEST2_OUTPUT_LVL)
69 ## --verbose, -v Shorthand for --output-lvl:VERBOSE
70 ##
71 ## Command line parsing can be disabled with `-d:unittest2DisableParamFiltering`.
72 ##
73 ## Running tests in parallel
74 ## =========================
75 ##
76 ## Early versions of this library had rudimentary support for running tests in
77 ## parallel - this has since been removed due to safety issues in the
78 ## implementation and may be reintroduced at a future date.
79 ##
80 ## Example
81 ## -------
82 ##
83 ## .. code:: nim
84 ##
85 ## suite "description for this stuff":
86 ## echo "suite setup: run once before the tests"
87 ##
88 ## setup:
89 ## echo "run before each test"
90 ##
91 ## teardown:
92 ## echo "run after each test"
93 ##
94 ## test "essential truths":
95 ## # give up and stop if this fails
96 ## require(true)
97 ##
98 ## test "slightly less obvious stuff":
99 ## # print a nasty message and move on, skipping
100 ## # the remainder of this block
101 ## check(1 != 1)
102 ## check("asd"[2] == 'd')
103 ##
104 ## test "out of bounds error is thrown on bad access":
105 ## let v = @[1, 2, 3] # you can do initialization here
106 ## expect(IndexError):
107 ## discard v[4]
108 ##
109 ## suiteTeardown:
110 ## echo "suite teardown: run once after the tests"
111
112 import std/[
113 macros, sequtils, sets, strutils, streams, tables, times, monotimes]
114
115 when defined(nimHasWarnBareExcept):
116 # In unit tests, we want to at least attempt to catch Exception no matter its
117 # UB
118 {.warning[BareExcept]: off.}
119
120 {.warning[LockLevel]: off.}
121
122 when declared(stdout):
123 import std/os
124
125 const useTerminal = declared(stdout) and not defined(js)
126
127 type
128 OutputLevel* = enum ## The output verbosity of the tests.
129 ✗ VERBOSE, ## Print as much as possible.
130 ✗ COMPACT ## Print failures and compact success information
131 ✗ FAILURES, ## Print only failures
132 ✗ NONE ## Print nothing.
133 ✗
134 const
135 ✗ outputLevelDefault = COMPACT
136 slowThreshold = initDuration(seconds = 5)
137
138 # `unittest` compatibility
139 nimUnittestOutputLevel {.strdefine.} = $outputLevelDefault
140 nimUnittestColor {.strdefine.} = "auto" ## auto|on|off
141 nimUnittestAbortOnError {.booldefine.} = false
142
143 # `unittest2` compile-time configuration options
144 unittest2DisableParamFiltering {.booldefine.} = false
145 ## Disables automatic command line argument parsing - parsing is available
146 ## via the `parseParameters` function instead
147 unittest2Compat {.booldefine.} = true # This will be disabled in the future
148 ## Compatibility mode for `unittest` for easier porting and improved
149 ## backwards compatibility - no stability guarantees
150 unittest2NoCollect {.booldefine.} = false
151 ## Disable test collection mode where tests are enumerated before they are
152 ## run - in particular, this affects the order in which tests and suites
153 ## have their bodies evaluated and disables several features that require
154 ## knowing how many tests will be executed - experimental feature
155 unittest2PreviewIsolate {.booldefine.} = false
156 ## Preview isolation mode where each test is run in a separate process - may
157 ## be removed in the future
158 unittest2Static* {.booldefine.} = false
159 ## Run tests at compile time as well - only a subset of functionality is
160 ## enabled at compile-time meaning that tests must be written
161 ## conservatively. `suite` features (`setup` etc) in particular are not
162 ## supported.
163 unittest2ListTests* {.booldefine.} = false
164 ## List tests at runtime (useful for test runners)
165
166 when useTerminal:
167 import std/terminal
168
169 const
170 collect = (not unittest2NoCollect and not unittest2Compat) or unittest2PreviewIsolate or unittest2ListTests
171 autoParseArgs = not unittest2DisableParamFiltering
172 isolate = unittest2PreviewIsolate
173
174 when isolate:
175 let
176 isolated = getEnv("UNITTEST2_ISOLATED") == "1"
177 ## Test is running in the isolated environment
178

181 try:
182 18 exitprocs.addExitProc(p)

188 Test = object
189 suiteName: string
190 testName: string
191 impl: proc(suite, name: string): TestStatus
192 lineInfo: int
193 filename: string

 54 TestStatus* = enum ## The status of a test when it is done.
196 OK,
197 18 FAILED,
198 18 SKIPPED
199
200 18 TestResult* = object
201 18 suiteName*: string
202 ## Name of the test suite that contains this test case.
203 18 testName*: string
204 18 ## Name of the test case
205 status*: TestStatus
206 ✗ duration*: Duration # How long the test took, in seconds
207 ✗ output*: string
208 errors*: string
209 54
210 OutputFormatter* = ref object of RootObj
211
212 ConsoleOutputFormatter* = ref object of OutputFormatter
213 colorOutput: bool
214 ## Have test results printed in color.
215 ## Default is `auto` depending on `isatty(stdout)`, or override it with
216 ## `-d:nimUnittestColor:auto|on|off`.
217 ##
218 ## Deprecated: Setting the environment variable `NIMTEST_COLOR` to `always`
219 ## or `never` changes the default for the non-js target to true or false respectively.
220 ## Deprecated: the environment variable `NIMTEST_NO_COLOR`, when set, changes the
221 ## default to true, if `NIMTEST_COLOR` is undefined.
222 outputLevel: OutputLevel
223 ## Set the verbosity of test results.
224 ## Default is `VERBOSE`, or override with:
225 ## `-d:nimUnittestOutputLevel:VERBOSE|FAILURES|NONE`.
226 ##
227 ## Deprecated: the `NIMTEST_OUTPUT_LVL` environment variable is set for the non-js target.

268 abortOnError* {.threadvar.}: bool

271 ## or override with `-d:nimUnittestAbortOnError:on|off`.

437 proc formatFraction(cur, total: int): string =
438 let
439 cur = $cur
440 total = $total
441 "[" & align(cur, max(0, maxStatusLen - total.len - 1)) & "/" & total & "]"

498 203 return
499
500 # In verbose mode, print a line when the test starts so that output can be
501 # correlated with the test that's currently running rather than misleadingly
502 # being printed just below the test that just finished running.
503 let
504 203 counter =
505 when collect:
506 try: formatFraction(formatter.curTest, formatter.tests[formatter.curSuiteName]) & " "
507 except CatchableError: ""
508 else:
509 ✗ formatStatus("Test")
510

512 ✗ stdout.styledWrite " ", fgBlue, alignLeft(counter, maxStatusLen + maxDurationLen + 7)

519 ✗ method failureOccurred*(formatter: ConsoleOutputFormatter,
520 checkpoints: seq[string], stackTrace: string) =
521 ✗ if stackTrace.len > 0:
522 ✗ formatter.errors.add(stackTrace)
523 ✗ formatter.errors.add("\n")
524 ✗ for msg in items(checkpoints):
525 ✗ formatter.errors.add(" ")
526 ✗ formatter.errors.add(msg)
527 ✗ formatter.errors.add("\n")

547 ✗ proc printFailureInfo(formatter: ConsoleOutputFormatter, testResult: TestResult) =
548 ✗ # Show how to re-run this test case
549 ✗ echo repeat('=', testResult.testName.len)
550 ✗ echo " ", getAppFilename2(), " ", quoteShell(testResult.suiteName & "::" & testResult.testName)
551 ✗ echo repeat('-', testResult.testName.len)
552
553 # Show the output
554 ✗ if testResult.output.len > 0:
555 ✗ echo testResult.output
556 ✗ if testResult.errors.len > 0:
557 ✗ echo testResult.errors
558
559 ✗ proc printTestResultStatus(formatter: ConsoleOutputFormatter, testResult: TestResult) =
560 let
561 ✗ status = formatStatus(testResult.status)
562 ✗ duration = formatDuration(testResult.duration)
563
564 formatter.write do:
565 ✗ stdout.styledWrite(
566 ✗ " ", styleBright, testResult.status.color, status, " ")
567 ✗ if testResult.duration > slowThreshold:
568 ✗ stdout.styledWrite styleBright, duration
569 ✗ else:
570 ✗ stdout.write(duration)
571 ✗ stdout.write " ", testResult.testName
572 ✗ do:
573 ✗ stdout.styledWrite " ", status, " ", duration, " ", testResult.testName
574 ✗ echo ""

694 ✗ proc xmlEscape(s: string): string =
695 ✗ result = newStringOfCap(s.len)
696 ✗ for c in items(s):
697 ✗ case c:
698 ✗ of '<': result.add("&lt;")
699 ✗ of '>': result.add("&gt;")
700 ✗ of '&': result.add("&amp;")
701 ✗ of '"': result.add("&quot;")
702 ✗ of '\'': result.add("&apos;")
703 ✗ else:
704 ✗ if ord(c) < 32:
705 ✗ result.add("&#" & $ord(c) & ';')
706 ✗ else:
707 ✗ result.add(c)
708
709 ✗ proc newJUnitOutputFormatter*(stream: Stream): JUnitOutputFormatter =
710 ✗ ## Creates a formatter that writes report to the specified stream in
711 ## JUnit format.
712 ## The ``stream`` is NOT closed automatically when the test are finished,
713 ## because the formatter has no way to know when all tests are finished.
714 ## You should invoke formatter.close() to finalize the report.
715 ✗ result = JUnitOutputFormatter(
716 ✗ stream: stream,
717 ✗ defaultSuite: JUnitSuite(name: "default"),
718 ✗ currentSuite: -1,
719 ✗ )
720 ✗ try:
721 ✗ stream.writeLine("<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>")
722

748 ✗ method testEnded*(formatter: JUnitOutputFormatter, testResult: TestResult) =

754 ✗ func toFloatSeconds(duration: Duration): float64 =
755 ✗ duration.inNanoseconds().float64 / 1_000_000_000.0
756 ✗
757 ✗ proc writeTest(s: Stream, test: JUnitTest) {.raises: [CatchableError].} =
758 let
759 ✗ time = test.result.duration.toFloatSeconds()
760 ✗ timeStr = time.formatFloat(ffDecimal, precision = 6)
761
762 ✗ s.writeLine("\t\t<testcase name=\"$#\" time=\"$#\">" % [
763 ✗ xmlEscape(test.name), timeStr])
764 ✗ case test.result.status
765 of TestStatus.OK:
766 discard
767 ✗ of TestStatus.SKIPPED:
768 ✗ s.writeLine("\t\t\t<skipped />")
769 ✗ of TestStatus.FAILED:
770 ✗ if test.error[0].len > 0:
771 ✗ s.writeLine("\t\t\t<error message=\"$#\">$#</error>" % [
772 ✗ xmlEscape(join(test.error[0], "\n")), xmlEscape(test.error[1])])
773
774 ✗ for failure in test.failures:
775 ✗ s.writeLine("\t\t\t<failure message=\"$#\">$#</failure>" %
776 ✗ [xmlEscape(failure[^1]), xmlEscape(join(failure[0..^2], "\n"))])
777
778 ✗ s.writeLine("\t\t</testcase>")
779 ✗
780 ✗ proc countTests(counts: var (int, int, int, int, float), suite: JUnitSuite) =
781 ✗ counts[0] += suite.tests.len()
782 ✗ for test in suite.tests:
783 ✗ counts[4] += test.result.duration.toFloatSeconds()
784 ✗ case test.result.status
785 of TestStatus.OK:
786 discard
787 ✗ of TestStatus.SKIPPED:
788 ✗ counts[3] += 1
789 ✗ of TestStatus.FAILED:
790 ✗ if test.error[0].len > 0:
791 ✗ counts[2] += 1
792 ✗ else:
793 ✗ counts[1] += 1
794
795 ✗ proc writeSuite(s: Stream, suite: JUnitSuite) {.raises: [CatchableError].} =
796 ✗ var counts: (int, int, int, int, float)
797 ✗ countTests(counts, suite)
798 ✗
799 ✗ let timeStr = counts[4].formatFloat(ffDecimal, precision = 6)
800
801 ✗ s.writeLine("\t" & """<testsuite name="$1" tests="$2" failures="$3" errors="$4" skipped="$5" time="$6">""" % [
802 ✗ xmlEscape(suite.name), $counts[0], $counts[1], $counts[2], $counts[3], timeStr])
803
804 ✗ for test in suite.tests.items():
805 ✗ s.writeTest(test)
806 ✗
807 ✗ s.writeLine("\t</testsuite>")
808 ✗
809 ✗ method testRunEnded*(formatter: JUnitOutputFormatter) =
810 ## Completes the report and closes the underlying stream.
811 ✗ let s = formatter.stream
812
813 when defined(nimHasWarnBareExcept):
814 {.warning[BareExcept]:off.}
815 try:
816 ✗ s.writeLine("<testsuites>")
817 ✗
818 ✗ for suite in formatter.suites.mitems():
819 ✗ s.writeSuite(suite)
820 ✗
821 ✗ if formatter.defaultSuite.tests.len() > 0:
822 ✗ s.writeSuite(formatter.defaultSuite)
823 ✗
824 ✗ s.writeLine("</testsuites>")
825 ✗ s.close()
826 ✗ except Exception as exc: # Work around Exception raised in stream
827 ✗ echo "Cannot write JUnit: ", exc.msg
828 ✗ quit 1
829 ✗
830 when defined(nimHasWarnBareExcept):
831 {.warning[BareExcept]:on.}

833 ✗ proc glob(matcher, filter: string): bool =
834 ## Globbing using a single `*`. Empty `filter` matches everything.
835 ✗ if filter.len == 0:
836 ✗ return true
837
838 ✗ if not filter.contains('*'):
839 ✗ return matcher == filter
840
841 ✗ let beforeAndAfter = filter.split('*', maxsplit=1)
842 ✗ if beforeAndAfter.len == 1:
843 # "foo*"
844 ✗ return matcher.startsWith(beforeAndAfter[0])
845
846 ✗ if matcher.len < filter.len - 1:
847 ✗ return false # "12345" should not match "123*345"
848
849 ✗ return matcher.startsWith(beforeAndAfter[0]) and matcher.endsWith(
850 ✗ beforeAndAfter[1])
851 ✗
852 ✗ proc matchFilter(suiteName, testName, filter: string): bool =
853 ✗ if filter == "":
854 ✗ return true
855 ✗ if testName == filter:
856 # corner case for tests containing "::" in their name
857 ✗ return true
858 ✗ let suiteAndTestFilters = filter.split("::", maxsplit=1)
859
860 ✗ if suiteAndTestFilters.len == 1:
861 # no suite specified
862 ✗ let testFilter = suiteAndTestFilters[0]
863 ✗ return glob(testName, testFilter)
864
865 ✗ return glob(suiteName, suiteAndTestFilters[0]) and
866 ✗ glob(testName, suiteAndTestFilters[1])
867 ✗
868 when defined(testing): export matchFilter

932 template suite*(nameParam: string, body: untyped) {.dirty.} =
933 ## Declare a test suite identified by `name` with optional ``setup``
934 ## and/or ``teardown`` section.
935 ##
936 ## A test suite is a series of one or more related tests sharing a
937 ## common fixture (``setup``, ``teardown``). The fixture is executed
938 ## for EACH test.
939 ##
940 ## .. code-block:: nim
941 ## suite "test suite for addition":
942 ## setup:
943 ## let result = 4
944 ##
945 ## test "2 + 2 = 4":
946 ## check(2+2 == result)
947 ##
948 ## test "(2 + -2) != 4":
949 ## check(2 + -2 != result)
950 ##
951 ## # No teardown needed
952 ##
953 ## The suite will run the individual test cases in the order in which
954 ## they were listed. With default global settings the above code prints:
955 ##
956 ## .. code-block::
957 ##
958 ## [Suite] test suite for addition
959 ## [OK] 2 + 2 = 4
960 ## [OK] (2 + -2) != 4
961 bind collect, currentSuite, suiteStarted, suiteEnded
962
963 block:
964 template setup(setupBody: untyped) {.dirty, used.} =
965 var testSetupIMPLFlag {.used.} = true
966 template testSetupIMPL: untyped {.dirty.} = setupBody
967
968 template teardown(teardownBody: untyped) {.dirty, used.} =
969 var testTeardownIMPLFlag {.used.} = true
970 template testTeardownIMPL: untyped {.dirty.} = teardownBody
971
972 template suiteTeardown(suiteTeardownBody: untyped) {.dirty, used.} =
973 var testSuiteTeardownIMPLFlag {.used.} = true
974 template testSuiteTeardownIMPL: untyped {.dirty.} = suiteTeardownBody
975
976 when nimvm:
977 discard
978 else:
979 29 let suiteName {.inject.} = nameParam
980 when not collect:
981 # TODO deal with suite nesting
982

1002 template checkpoint*(msg: string) =
1003 ## Set a checkpoint identified by `msg`. Upon test failure all
1004 ## checkpoints encountered so far are printed out. Example:
1005 ##
1006 ## .. code-block:: nim
1007 ##
1008 ## checkpoint("Checkpoint A")
1009 ## check((42, "the Answer to life and everything") == (1, "a"))
1010 ## checkpoint("Checkpoint B")
1011 ##
1012 ## outputs "Checkpoint A" once it fails.
1013 when nimvm:
1014 {.cast(gcsafe).}:
1015 checkpointsVm.add msg
1016 else:
1017 bind checkpoints
1018
1019 ✗ checkpoints.add(msg)
1020 ✗ # TODO: add support for something like SCOPED_TRACE from Google Test
1021
1022 template fail* =
1023 ## Print out the checkpoints encountered so far and quit if ``abortOnError``
1024 ## is true. Otherwise, erase the checkpoints and indicate the test has
1025 ## failed (change exit code and test status). This template is useful
1026 ## for debugging, but is otherwise mostly used internally. Example:
1027 ##
1028 ## .. code-block:: nim
1029 ##
1030 ## checkpoint("Checkpoint A")
1031 ## complicatedProcInThread()
1032 ## fail()
1033 ##
1034 ## outputs "Checkpoint A" before quitting.
1035 when nimvm:
1036 echo "Tests failed"
1037 {.cast(gcsafe).}:
1038 for msg in items(checkpointsVm):
1039 echo(" ")
1040 echo(msg)
1041 echo("\n")
1042 quit 1
1043 else:

1132 try:
1133 block:
1134 code
1135 ✗ except CatchableError as e:
1136 ✗ prefix.fail("error", e)

1139 ✗ except Exception as e:

1141 ✗ when NimMajor>=2:
1142 {.pop.}

 203
1177 template staticTest*(nameParam: string, body: untyped) =
1178 ## Similar to `test` but runs only at compiletime, no matter the
1179 ## `unittest2Static` flag
1180 static:
1181 block:
1182 echo "[Test ] ", nameParam
1183 body
1184 echo "[", TestStatus.OK, " ] ", nameParam
1185 {.cast(gcsafe).}:
1186 reset checkpointsVm
1187
1188 template dualTest*(nameParam: string, body: untyped) =
1189 ## Similar to `test` but run the test both compuletime and run time, no
1190 ## matter the `unittest2Static` flag
1191 staticTest nameParam:
1192 when not unittest2ListTests:
1193 body
1194 runtimeTest nameParam:
1195 when not unittest2ListTests:
1196 body
1197
1198 template test*(nameParam: string, body: untyped) =
1199 ## Define a single test case identified by `name`.
1200 ##
1201 ## .. code-block:: nim
1202 ##
1203 ## test "roses are red":
1204 ## let roses = "red"
1205 ## check(roses == "red")
1206 ##
1207 ## The above code outputs:
1208 ##
1209 ## .. code-block::
1210 ##
1211 ## [OK] roses are red
1212 when nimvm:
1213 when unittest2Static:
1214 staticTest nameParam:
1215 when not unittest2ListTests:
1216 body
1217 runtimeTest nameParam:
1218 when not unittest2ListTests:
1219 body
1220
1221 {.pop.} # raises: []
1222
1223

 361 iterator unittest2EvalOnceIter[T](x: T): auto =
1224 yield x
1225 iterator unittest2EvalOnceIter[T](x: var T): var T =
1226 yield x
1227
1228 template unittest2EvalOnce(name: untyped, param: typed, blk: untyped) =
1229 for name in unittest2EvalOnceIter(param):
1230 blk
1231
1232 macro check*(conditions: untyped): untyped =
1233 ## Verify if a statement or a list of statements is true.
1234 ## A helpful error message and set checkpoints are printed out on
1235 ## failure (if ``outputLevel`` is not ``NONE``).
1236 runnableExamples:
1237 import std/strutils
1238
1239 check("AKB48".toLowerAscii() == "akb48")
1240
1241 let teams = {'A', 'K', 'B', '4', '8'}
1242
1243 check:
1244 "AKB48".toLowerAscii() == "akb48"
1245 'C' notin teams
1246
1247 {.warning[Deprecated]:off.}
1248 let checked = callsite()[1]
1249 {.warning[Deprecated]:on.}
1250
1251 template print(name: untyped, value: typed) =
1252 when compiles(string($value)):
1253

✗ Branch 609 not taken.
 313 checkpoint(name & " was " & $value)
1254 ✗
1255 proc inspectArgs(exp: NimNode): tuple[frame, inner, check, printOuts: NimNode] =
1256 result.check = copyNimTree(exp)
1257 result.inner = newNimNode(nnkStmtList)
1258 result.printOuts = newNimNode(nnkStmtList)
1259
1260 var counter = 0
1261 25 let evalOnce = bindSym("unittest2EvalOnce")
1262 result.frame = result.inner
1263 if exp[0].kind in {nnkIdent, nnkOpenSymChoice, nnkClosedSymChoice, nnkSym} and
1264 $exp[0] in ["not", "in", "notin", "==", "<=",
1265 ">=", "<", ">", "!=", "is", "isnot"]:
1266
1267 for i in 1 ..< exp.len:
1268 if exp[i].kind notin nnkLiterals:
1269 inc counter
1270 let argStr = exp[i].toStrLit
1271 let paramAst = exp[i]
1272 if exp[i].kind == nnkIdent:
1273 35 result.printOuts.add getAst(print(argStr, paramAst))
1274 if exp[i].kind in nnkCallKinds + {nnkDotExpr, nnkBracketExpr, nnkPar} and
1275 (exp[i].typeKind notin {ntyTypeDesc} or $exp[0] notin ["is", "isnot"]):
1276 let callVar = newIdentNode(":c" & $counter)
1277 result.frame = nnkCall.newTree(evalOnce, callVar, paramAst, result.frame)
1278 result.check[i] = callVar
1279 68 result.printOuts.add getAst(print(argStr, callVar))
1280 if exp[i].kind == nnkExprEqExpr:
1281 # ExprEqExpr
1282 # Ident "v"
1283 # IntLit 2
1284 result.check[i] = exp[i][1]
1285 if exp[i].typeKind notin {ntyTypeDesc}:
1286 let arg = newIdentNode(":p" & $counter)
1287 result.frame = nnkCall.newTree(evalOnce, arg, paramAst, result.frame)
1288 result.printOuts.add getAst(print(argStr, arg))
1289 if exp[i].kind != nnkExprEqExpr:
1290 result.check[i] = arg
1291 else:
1292 result.check[i][1] = arg
1293
1294 proc buildCheck(lineinfo, callLit, check, printOuts: NimNode): NimNode =
1295 let
1296 checkpointSym = bindSym("checkpoint")
1297 failSym = bindSym("fail")
1298 nnkBlockStmt.newTree(
1299 newEmptyNode(),
1300 nnkStmtList.newTree(
1301 nnkIfStmt.newTree(
1302 nnkElifBranch.newTree(
1303 nnkCall.newTree(ident("not"), check),
1304 nnkStmtList.newTree(
1305 nnkCall.newTree(
1306 checkpointSym,
1307 nnkInfix.newTree(
1308 ident("&"),
1309 nnkInfix.newTree(
1310 ident("&"),
1311 lineinfo,
1312 newLit(": Check failed: ")
1313 ),
1314 callLit
1315 )
1316 ),
1317 printOuts,
1318 nnkCall.newTree(failSym)
1319 )
1320 )
1321 )
1322 )
1323 )
1324
1325 let
1326 checkSym = bindSym("check")
1327
1328 case checked.kind
1329 of nnkCallKinds:
1330 let
1331 (frame, inner, check, printOuts) = inspectArgs(checked)
1332 lineinfo = newStrLitNode(checked.lineInfo)
1333 callLit = checked.toStrLit
1334
1335 inner.add buildCheck(lineinfo, callLit, check, printOuts)
1336 result = frame
1337 of nnkStmtList:
1338 result = newNimNode(nnkStmtList)
1339 for node in checked:
1340 if node.kind != nnkCommentStmt:
1341 result.add(newCall(checkSym, node))
1342
1343 else:
1344 let
1345 lineinfo = newStrLitNode(checked.lineInfo)
1346 callLit = checked.toStrLit
1347
1348 result = buildCheck(
1349 lineinfo, callLit, checked, newEmptyNode())
1350
1351 template require*(conditions: untyped) =
1352 ## Same as `check` except any failed test causes the program to quit
1353 ## immediately. Any teardown statements are not executed and the failed
1354 ## test output is not generated.
1355 when nimvm:
1356 check conditions
1357 else:
1358 let savedAbortOnError = abortOnError
1359 block:
1360 abortOnError = true
1361 check conditions
1362 abortOnError = savedAbortOnError
1363
1364 macro expect*(exceptions: varargs[typed], body: untyped): untyped =
1365 ## Test if `body` raises an exception found in the passed `exceptions`.
1366 ## The test passes if the raised exception is part of the acceptable
1367 ## exceptions. Otherwise, it fails.
1368 runnableExamples:
1369 import std/[math, random, strutils]
1370 proc defectiveRobot() =
1371 randomize()
1372 case rand(1..4)
1373 of 1: raise newException(OSError, "CANNOT COMPUTE!")
1374 of 2: discard parseInt("Hello World!")
1375 of 3: raise newException(IOError, "I can't do that Dave.")
1376 else: assert 2 + 2 == 5
1377
1378 expect IOError, OSError, ValueError, AssertionDefect:
1379 defectiveRobot()
1380
1381 template expectBody(errorTypes, lineInfoLit, body): NimNode {.dirty.} =
1382 try:
1383 try:
1384 body
1385 checkpoint(lineInfoLit & ": Expect Failed, no exception was thrown.")
1386 fail()
1387 except errorTypes:
1388 discard
1389 except CatchableError as e:
1390 checkpoint(lineInfoLit & ": Expect Failed, unexpected " & $e.name &
1391 " (" & e.msg & ") was thrown.\n" & e.getStackTrace())
1392 fail()
1393 except Defect as e:
1394 checkpoint(lineInfoLit & ": Expect Failed, unexpected " & $e.name &
1395 " (" & e.msg & ") was thrown.\n" & e.getStackTrace())
1396 fail()
1397
1398 var errorTypes = newNimNode(nnkBracket)
1399 for exp in exceptions:
1400 errorTypes.add(exp)
1401
1402 result = getAst(expectBody(errorTypes, errorTypes.lineInfo, body))
1403
1404 proc disableParamFiltering* {.deprecated:
1405 "Compile with -d:unittest2DisableParamFiltering instead".} =
1406 discard
1407
1408 when unittest2PreviewIsolate:
1409 import std/[osproc, strtabs]
1410 proc runIsolated(test: Test) =
1411 # Run test in an isolated process - this has the advantage that we can
1412 # trivially capture stdout but has a number of problems:
1413 # * suite and other global stuff gets executed for each test
1414 # * on unix, `fork` could work around this but not on windows
1415 # * there's no good way to separate errors from stdout
1416 # * there's process overhead
1417 #
1418 # There are advantages too:
1419 # * reduced cross-test pollution
1420 # * simple to parallelise
1421 # * we can abort long-running tests after a timeout
1422
1423 let startTime = getMonoTime()
1424 testStarted(test.testName)
1425
1426 let runner = startProcess(
1427 getAppFilename2(),
1428 args = [test.suiteName & "::" & test.testName],
1429 env = newStringTable(
1430 "UNITTEST2_ISOLATED", "1",
1431 StringTableMode.modeCaseSensitive),
1432 options = {poStdErrToStdOut})
1433
1434 close(runner.inputStream) # EOF so the test doesn't think it'll get input
1435
1436 var output: string
1437
1438 while true:
1439 let pos = output.len
1440 output.setLen(pos + 4096)
1441
1442 let bytes = runner.outputStream.readData(addr output[pos], 4096)
1443 if bytes >= 0:
1444 output.setLen(pos + bytes)
1445
1446 if bytes <= 0:
1447 break
1448
1449 let status = runner.waitForExit()
1450
1451 runner.close()
1452
1453 testEnded(TestResult(
1454 suiteName: test.suiteName,
1455 testName: test.testName,
1456 status: if status == 0: TestStatus.OK else: TestStatus.FAILED,
1457 duration: getMonoTime() - startTime,
1458 output: output
1459 ))
1460
1461 type
1462 IsolatedFormatter* = ref object of OutputFormatter
1463 ## Formatter suitable for using the process-isolated environment
1464 ##
1465 ## This is a work in progress with several open issues
1466 ## * we could use stderr for "unittest" traffic but it would be
1467 ## compromised by application output (typically ok in nim) and makes
1468 ## reading messy
1469 ## * we could print all errors after test providing some sort of
1470 ## separator - has escape issues
1471 ## * we could redirect stdout/stderr to a file and use stdout for errors
1472 ## * as an addon to the above, we could read back the file then print
1473 ## a structured test format to stdout which the parent process can
1474 ## capture easily
1475
1476 if isolated:
1477 formatters.add(IsolatedFormatter())
1478
1479 method failureOccurred*(formatter: IsolatedFormatter,
1480 checkpoints: seq[string], stackTrace: string) =
1481 if stackTrace.len > 0:
1482 echo(stackTrace)
1483 echo("\n")
1484 for msg in items(checkpoints):
1485 echo(" ")
1486 echo(msg)
1487 echo("\n")
1488
1489 when collect:
1490 proc runScheduledTests() {.noconv.} =
1491 # Tests can be added inside tests - this is weird and only partially
1492 # supported
1493 while tests.len > 0:
1494 var tmp = move(tests)
1495 when unittest2ListTests:
1496 for suiteName, suite in tmp:
1497 if suite.len == 0: continue
1498 echo "Suite: ", suiteName
1499 for test in suite:
1500 echo "\tTest: ", test.testName
1501 echo "\tFile: ", test.filename, ":", test.lineInfo
1502 else:
1503 suiteRunStarted(tmp)
1504 for suiteName, suite in tmp:
1505 if suite.len == 0: continue
1506
1507 suiteStarted(suiteName)
1508 for test in suite:
1509 when isolate:
1510 if not isolated:
1511 runIsolated(test)
1512 else:
1513 runDirect(test)
1514 else:
1515 runDirect(test)
1516
1517 suiteEnded()
1518
1519 suiteRunEnded()
1520 when not unittest2ListTests:
1521 testRunEnded()
1522
1523 addExitProc(runScheduledTests)
1524
1525 else:
1526 18 addExitProc(proc() {.noconv.} = testRunEnded())
1527
Created using GCOVR (Version 8.5) at 2026-06-06 00:35:57
